f375c0feef04998d491c6cc0a9f8d28514e633b3: Departmental guidelines from Dept of IOT &IS mandate that Final Year B.Tech students must complete their work within a minimum duration of 16 weeks by appointing faculty mentors or industry professionals as external supervisors based on whether the assignment is internal or external.

Faculty mentors serve as students' supervisors by guiding projects through tentative titles/domain lists and encouraging faculty contact for guidance on current knowledge using tools/results by Jan 24, 2026. Students must allocate dedicated lab/classrooms under faculty supervision before this deadline to ensure their work is completed effectively.

Internal students must attend class twice daily between 9:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m., receive room allocation under faculty guidance, mark attendance in the register with the major project coordinator, and report on project offers until January 31st while adhering to registration form submission deadlines notified by academic sections for internal students.

Internal students must submit Registration forms starting from 8th semester commencement and maintain regular attendance with mandatory participation rates of 75% during final submission at end of 8th semester, while also completing Synopses per deadline or receiving extension if they do not meet the 4-month requirement set by their supervisor.

Students must complete projects within four months with extension options; group work can be done individually or as a team of two students, with reports including an 'implementation plan' and supervisors needing to sign off on weekly observation meetings.

Students must meet supervisors to discuss progress and obtain signatures on observation sheets for Mid-terms and End-Semester examinations, while ensuring reports are originalized without plagiarism with proper citations for prior work before submission.
